name: AG Anti-Affinity (Masters HA)
goal: Prove solver respects AG anti-affinity spreading while round-robin does not.
hypothesis: RR iterates BMs in listing order and places the first 3 masters into the first 3 BMs; because bm-s1-01 and bm-s1-02 share ag-s1-1, RR produces an AG-collision (2 masters in the same AG). Solver guarantees 1 master per AG.

inventory_setup.description: Create 3 AGs, 6 racks (2 per AG), 6 empty BMs (1 per rack). No placeholder VMs.

anti_affinity_rules.note: If v2 auto-generates anti-affinity from node_role=master, this rule is redundant but harmless. Include it for v2 to be explicit. v1 likely ignores it.

execution_steps: 1. Create ags/racks/bms in inventory via inventory API.
2. Confirm no VMs exist on any bm-s1-* before starting.
3. Call POST /api/v1/schedule with the test_request (RR). Record assignment.
4. Delete the 6 test VMs created by v1 (or reset the scenario BMs to empty).
5. Call POST /api/v2/scheduler with the same test_request (solver). Record assignment.
6. Report back using the template in reporting_template.
metrics: master_ag_distribution: count of masters per AG (derived from mapping + bm->rack->ag)
anti_affinity_violations: number of AGs where master count > max_per_ag (=1)
placement_success: all 6 VMs placed, no unplaced
bm_count_used: distinct BMs holding at least one test VM
reporting_template:
Scenario: S1
API: v1
Status: OK
Mapping:
  vm-s1-m1 -> bm-s1-??
  vm-s1-m2 -> bm-s1-??
  vm-s1-m3 -> bm-s1-??
  vm-s1-w1 -> bm-s1-??
  vm-s1-w2 -> bm-s1-??
  vm-s1-w3 -> bm-s1-??
Unplaced: none

---

Scenario: S1
API: v2
Status: OK
Mapping:
  vm-s1-m1 -> bm-s1-??
  ... (same 6 VMs)
Unplaced: none
